title: Super Mario Odyssey
edition_pinned: Super Mario Odyssey (Nintendo Switch, October 2017 launch) — Capture and Power Moon kingdom structure as shipped; not post-launch costume DLC focus.
moat: Capture converts enemies and objects into temporary move-sets that open moons the base kit cannot reach.

loop_skeleton.description: Traverse a kingdom with Mario's base moves, throw Cappy to capture a target, use the capture's move-set to reach a Power Moon, bank moons to fuel the Odyssey to the next kingdom when the exit threshold is met.

depth_mechanisms.name: capture move-set swap
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Throwing Cappy onto a legal target replaces Mario's actions with that target's kit (swim, fly, roll, possess object). Most captures last until the player releases them or a capture-specific end condition fires; only some captures are time-limited. Moons are authored around capture tools as first-class solutions, with many also solvable by advanced base movement.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If Capture is removed and only Mario's base kit remains, moons that require a capture-specific action cannot be completed by the base kit alone.

depth_mechanisms.name: over-supplied moon threshold
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Each kingdom offers more Power Moons than the Odyssey exit cost, so players choose which moons to bank rather than following a single mandatory path.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If moon count equals the exact exit requirement with no surplus, second-session exploration of optional moons stops because every moon becomes a choke rather than a menu of options.

depth_mechanisms.name: capture-as-exploration bait
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Kingdoms seed large capturable objects and creatures that advertise new experiences (dinosaur, vehicle-like objects) so exploration seeks new capture toys, not only coin-shaped collectibles.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If capturable spectacle targets are removed and only coin and platform moons remain, sessions stop introducing new move-set experiences and reduce to pure collectathon routing.

feel_signature: A throw attaches Cappy to a target; success is a new body with different jump, attack, or traversal rules, then a moon bank that advances the Odyssey exit meter.
second_session_answer: Players return to farm surplus moons in known kingdoms, reusing world-local capturable targets and post-game moon types, not a persistent unlocked capture roster or a linear boss gauntlet.
packaging_lessons: Inference: ship one kingdom with base kit plus a small capture roster before stacking multiplayer or post-game moon variants.
Set exit moon costs below available moon supply so the first clear teaches choice, not completionism.
anti_lessons: Do not treat Capture as a skin swap — if the captured form does not change legal routes or attacks, it is costume, not depth.
Do not copy moon spam without multi-solution placement; quantity without alternate solves is checklist padding.

system_bom.note: jump, throw, capture, and moon bank form the core cycle that converts kingdom exploration into Odyssey fuel
Power Moon totals unlock Odyssey travel and later kingdoms so collection is the travel gate
world-local capture targets expand legal move-sets without a traditional skill tree or permanent unlock list
kingdom map and moon list support optional tracking; uncollected moons are not universally marked until hints or list checks are used
banked moons, story flags, and checkpoint progress persist; captures are not a persistent unlocked roster
fall or damage returns Mario with partial coin loss; moons already banked stay banked

citations.claim: Moat and capture move-set swap: Capture controls enemies, creatures, and objects via Cappy to gain new abilities and reach Power Moons.
Capture duration: captures generally last until release or capture-specific end conditions, not a universal timed window for every target.
Over-supplied moon threshold: Power Moons power the Odyssey; kingdoms contain more moons than the exit requirement.
Progression BOM: Power Moons are required to power the Odyssey and travel between kingdoms.
Capture-as-exploration bait: designers describe shifting from pure star search to searching for new captures and experiences.
Feel signature: Cappy throw enables Capture, which changes movement and combat options used to reach moons.
Feel signature moon bank advances kingdom exit: collected Power Moons fund Odyssey travel.
Second-session answer: surplus moons and post-story collection remain in kingdoms; captures remain world-local targets rather than a permanent unlock roster.
Skills BOM: Capture expands move-sets per target without a traditional skill tree.
UI BOM: moon lists and maps support tracking, but moons are not all forced into a single ordered path.
Save-state BOM: banked moons and kingdom progress persist across sessions; captures are not stored as unlocked abilities.
Win-loss-death BOM: death costs coins while previously collected Power Moons remain collected.
Play-loop BOM: jump, Capture, and moon collection form the kingdom cycle that feeds Odyssey travel.
Packaging lesson 1 (labeled Inference): one kingdom plus Capture roster is the minimal vertical slice before post-game moon variants.
Packaging lesson 2: exit costs sit below available moons so the first clear teaches selection among surplus moons.
Director interview context: Power Moons hidden throughout kingdoms and Capture as a core exploration tool.
Anti-lesson Capture-as-costume: Capture is defined by gained abilities and new routes, not appearance alone.
Anti-lesson moon quantity without multi-solution: designer coverage stresses experiences and Capture solves, not checklist count alone.
